## [00:00 - 03:00]

So this video is one of the kind of secrets to how we maintain so much control over our ad accounts. So this dashboard that you're seeing right now is generated with data that's obtained from Facebook but allows us to see the data without checking in on Facebook. Now the data that you see in front of you is based on how we like to view the data. So if you've been through the KPIs part of the training you'll understand how and why I've structured these. So this first set of data what I call the top level KPIs these are the main metrics that I'm looking at every time I look at this dashboard. So we I set this up for every single ad account that I'm running. So here for example this is one ad account and you can see the the data for these stats the data for this particular account and we will have you know five, 10, 15, 20 different ad accounts stacked up in a spreadsheet like this and we pull the data currently automatically through the third party. Now you can download the data manually yourself from Facebook like I used to do many years ago and then get it into the format that you can read it into these columns using calculations. So if I hover over if I click into this one here there's some calculations that are pulling data from a raw data tab in this spreadsheet and matching up some data so that I can view my ad account information. What that does is first of all when it pulls the data it's formatting it in a way that I want. So if you've been through previous training you'll know that average order value is not something that you can see automatically in Facebook ads reporting but when I can control and manipulate the data as I want I can display it how I want. Secondly what I find really really useful is using color coding to determine good and bad results. So here for example I've just simply used some conditional formatting that's that looks at how high and low the numbers are comparatively and gives them a color grading. Now one thing that I do is I look at the last 14 days in this particular view you can do 30 days you can do 60 don't even need to do it by day if you want it's just something that I prefer to do as my kind of daily check-in it's called our daily Facebook ads dashboard and what I like to do is look at the color grading but I put my benchmark numbers in this row here so my benchmark ROAS for this particular ad account is I'm aiming for 133% ROAS and this is a front-end return on ad spend so that means when I spend a dollar as long as I get $1.33 back I'm happy because I know on my back end once I go through my upsells and my lifetime value

I'm highly profitable so I know that 133% is good and what the conditional formatting does is I select these columns and create conditional formatting and what it does is if the numbers are you know level metrics right there then if I look at my scroll down and find our table here so my secondary kpi's are then looking at my funnel so what I like to do is although I'm looking at my main kpi's and figuring out what's working what's not etc etc I now want to look at my funnel metrics and again I've got benchmarks so I've got benchmarks I put in place so I know what a good cosper view content is a good cosper ad to car is good click through right and again I can scan down and see which days I'm performing better or worse than others I can look at my conversion from click to landing page to view content ad to car initiate checkout and sell for this one there isn't really an initiate checkout event that fires but that's kind of how I would analyze my results and I what I've done is to create a template for you and in the next video to show you how to connect your data up to create something similar as well so in the template you'll have the everything will be connected to your raw data tab it will push into a tab similar to this we can actually pull your data through the easiest way to do it is to take your data and export it from ad manager but the more scalable way of doing it is to use a third-party tool which I'll explain in another video as well
